query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: Broad chemical resistance (acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, salt solutions)
Good flexibility at room temperature (elastic modulus < 1 MPa)
Transparency in the visible spectrum (>80% transmittance)
constraints: Must be free of per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (no fluorine bonds)
Compatible with typical lab manufacturing equipment
No hazardous crosslinking agents that require special handling

final_candidate.justification: The 3‑2‑1 silicone formulation is a commercially available, non‑PFAS elastomer that offers excellent resistance to a wide range of acids, bases, organic solvents, oils and saline solutions. It has an elastic modulus around 0.5 MPa at room temperature, providing flexibility comparable to THV. The material can be cast or molded in clear form, giving >80 % visible‑light transmittance. Silicone elastomers exhibit low gas permeability relative to most thermoplastics (≈10^-12 mol·m⁻¹·s⁻¹·Pa⁻¹) and are stable up to ~200 °C, meeting the thermal requirement.

process_recipe.description: Prepare base silicone (polydimethylsiloxane) and vinyl‑terminated crosslinker in a clean mixing vessel.
process_recipe.conditions: Room temperature, gentle stirring to avoid foaming.

process_recipe.description: Add platinum catalyst (Karstedt’s catalyst) at 0.05 wt% of total silicone weight.
process_recipe.conditions: Maintain temperature <30 °C during addition to prevent premature curing.

process_recipe.description: Mix thoroughly until homogeneous; then degas under vacuum (≈0.1 bar) for 5–10 min to remove entrained air.
Pour the degassed mixture into a pre‑cleaned mold or cast onto a flat substrate (e.g., glass plate).
process_recipe.conditions: Room temperature, avoid contact with metal surfaces that may catalyze crosslinking.

process_recipe.description: Cure the silicone in an oven at 150 °C for 2 h (or until the mixture is fully crosslinked).
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature ramp of 10 °C/min, hold time 2 h.

process_recipe.description: Cool to room temperature and demold the cured elastomer. Inspect for clarity and surface finish.
